en: Seeing the crowds, he went up on the mountain, and when he sat down, his disciples came to him.
zh: 耶穌看見群眾，就上了山；他坐下之後，門徒來到他跟前，

en: And he opened his mouth and taught them, saying:
zh: 他就開口教訓他們：

en: "Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
zh: “心靈貧乏的人有福了， 因為天國是他們的。

en: "Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
zh: 哀痛的人有福了， 因為他們必得安慰。

en: "Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
zh: 溫柔的人有福了， 因為他們必承受地土。

en: "Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.
zh: 愛慕公義如飢如渴的人有福了， 因為他們必得飽足。

en: "Blessed are the merciful, for they shall receive mercy.
zh: 憐憫人的人有福了， 因為他們必蒙憐憫。

en: "Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
zh: 內心清潔的人有福了， 因為他們必看見 神。

en: "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.
zh: 使人和平的人有福了， 因為他們必稱為 神的兒子。

en: "Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
zh: 為義遭受迫害的人有福了， 因為天國是他們的。

en: "Blessed are you when others revile you and persecute you and utter all kinds of evil against you falsely on my account.
zh: 人若因我的緣故辱罵你們，迫害你們，並且捏造各樣壞話毀謗你們，你們就有福了。

en: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
zh: 你們應該歡喜快樂，因為你們在天上的賞賜是大的；在你們以前的先知，他們也曾這樣迫害。

en: "You are the salt of the earth, but if salt has lost its taste, how shall its saltiness be restored? It is no longer good for anything except to be thrown out and trampled under people's feet.
zh: “你們是地上的鹽；如果鹽失了味，怎能使它再鹹呢？結果毫無用處，唯有丟在外面任人踐踏。

en: "You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden.
zh: 你們是世上的光。建在山上的城是無法隱藏的；

en: Nor do people light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a stand, and it gives light to all in the house.
zh: 人點了燈，不會放在量器底下，而是放在燈臺上，就照亮一家人。

en: In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.
zh: 照樣，你們的光也應當照在人前，讓他們看見你們的好行為，又頌讚你們在天上的父。

en: "Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.
zh: “你們不要以為我來是要廢除律法和先知；我來不是要廢除，而是要完成。

en: For truly, I say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, not an iota, not a dot, will pass from the Law until all is accomplished.
zh: 我實在告訴你們，就算天地過去，律法的一點一畫也不會廢去，全部都要成就。

en: Therefore whoever relaxes one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do the same will be called least in the kingdom of heaven, but whoever does them and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
zh: 因此，無論誰廢除誡命中最小的一條，又這樣教導人，他在天國中必稱為最小的；但若有人遵行這些誡命，並且教導人遵行，他在天國中必稱為大。

en: For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
zh: 我告訴你們，你們的義若不勝過經學家和法利賽人的義，就必不能進天國。

en: "You have heard that it was said to those of old, 'You shall not murder; and whoever murders will be liable to judgment.'
zh: “你們聽過有這樣吩咐古人的話：‘不可殺人，殺人的必被判罪。’

en: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment; whoever insults his brother will be liable to the council; and whoever says, 'You fool!' will be liable to the hell of fire.
zh: 可是我告訴你們，凡是向弟兄發怒的，必被判罪。人若說弟兄是‘拉加’，必被公議會審判；人若說弟兄是‘摩利’，必難逃地獄的火。

en: So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
zh: 所以你在祭壇上獻供物的時候，如果在那裡想起你的弟兄對你不滿，

en: leave your gift there before the altar and go. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
zh: 就當在壇前放下供物，先去與弟兄和好，然後才來獻你的供物。

en: Come to terms quickly with your accuser while you are going with him to court, lest your accuser hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you be put in prison.
zh: 趁著你和你的對頭還在路上的時候，要趕快與他和解，免得他抓你去見法官，法官把你交給獄警，關在監裡。

en: Truly, I say to you, you will never get out until you have paid the last penny.
zh: 我實在告訴你，除非你還清最後一分錢，否則決不能從那裡出來。

en: "You have heard that it was said, 'You shall not commit adultery.'
zh: “你們聽過有這樣的吩咐：‘不可姦淫。’

en: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lustful intent has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
zh: 可是我告訴你們，凡是看見婦女就動淫念的，心裡已經犯了姦淫。

en: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of your members than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
zh: 如果你的右眼使你犯罪，就把它挖出來丟掉；寧可失去身體的一部分，勝過全身被丟進地獄裡。

en: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of your members than that your whole body go into hell.
zh: 如果你的右手使你犯罪，就把它砍下來丟掉；寧可失去身體的一部分，勝過全身進到地獄裡去。

en: "It was also said, 'Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.'
zh: “又有這樣的吩咐：‘人若休妻，就應當給她休書。’

en: But I say to you that everyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of sexual immorality, makes her commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
zh: 可是我告訴你們，凡休妻的，如果不是因她不貞，就是促使她犯姦淫；無論誰娶了被休的婦人，也就是犯姦淫了。

en: "Again you have heard that it was said to those of old, 'You shall not swear falsely, but shall perform to the Lord what you have sworn.'
zh: “你們又聽過有這樣吩咐古人的話：‘不可背約，向主許的願都要償還。’

en: But I say to you, Do not take an oath at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
zh: 可是我告訴你們，總不可發誓，不可指著天發誓，因為天是 神的寶座；

en: or by the earth, for it is his footstool, or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
zh: 不可指著地發誓，因為地是 神的腳凳；不可指著耶路撒冷發誓，因為它是大君王的京城；

en: And do not take an oath by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
zh: 也不可指著自己的頭發誓，因為你不能使一根頭髮變白或變黑。

en: Let what you say be simply 'Yes' or 'No'; anything more than this comes from evil.
zh: 你們的話，是就說‘是’，不是就說‘不是’；如果再多說，就是出於那惡者。

en: "You have heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.'
zh: “你們聽過有這樣的吩咐：‘以眼還眼，以牙還牙。’

en: But I say to you, Do not resist the one who is evil. But if anyone slaps you on the right cheek, turn to him the other also.
zh: 可是我告訴你們，不要與惡人對抗，有人打你的右臉，把另一邊也轉過來讓他打；

en: And if anyone would sue you and take your tunic, let him have your cloak as well.
zh: 有人要告你，想拿你的襯衫，就連外套也讓他拿去。

en: And if anyone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
zh: 有人要強迫你走一里路，就陪他走兩里。

en: Give to the one who begs from you, and do not refuse the one who would borrow from you.
zh: 有求你的，就給他；想借貸的，也不可拒絕。

en: "You have heard that it was said, 'You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.'
zh: “你們聽過有這樣的吩咐：‘當愛你的鄰舍，恨你的仇敵。’

en: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
zh: 可是我告訴你們，當愛你們的仇敵，為迫害你們的祈禱，

en: so that you may be sons of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the just and on the unjust.
zh: 好叫你們成為你們天父的兒子；因為他使太陽照惡人，也照好人；降雨給義人，也給不義的人。

en: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
zh: 如果你們只愛那些愛你們的人，有甚麼賞賜呢？稅吏不也是這樣作嗎？

en: And if you greet only your brothers, what more are you doing than others? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
zh: 如果你們單問候你們的弟兄，有甚麼特別呢？教外人不也是這樣作嗎？

en: You therefore must be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
zh: 所以你們要完全，正如你們的天父是完全的。”
